behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (classifier -> API client payload handoff), not only inside a pure helper. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network needed; an in-process fake client that captures outbound payload is sufficient. LLM-DEP: does not depend on model judgment, only request content. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is about request construction ('includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels'), not live provider behavior or output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with a fake API client, call classify(), and assert the captured outbound request contains every taxonomy label exactly as configured (including ordering/format requirements if specified).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is deterministic normalization/guard logic on one response value. No cross-module state propagation is required to trigger the bug. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none for this behavior because input is a fixed raw label fixture. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration+ because behavior text focuses on mapping an unexpected label to 'unknown', which is local logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/decision function with taxonomy plus an out-of-taxonomy label fixture and assert final output is exactly 'unknown'.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior explicitly scopes to normalization determinism for identical input; this is pure function/object behavior. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none because raw response is fixed. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration+ because no module handoff is required by the behavior text ('identical raw API responses' -> same normalized label).
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization multiple times on the same raw response fixture (table/property style) and assert identical output label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model/provider outputs across repeated live calls; a stub would hide true consistency failures. REAL-INFRA exists, but the decisive boundary is model behavior variability/decisioning, so Agentic is the minimum. MULTI-STEP: repeated calls are repeated single-step classifications, not a chained multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text does not describe sequential agentic stages with inter-step handoff; only repeated calls to one classifier decision.
behaviors.test_description: Against the live provider, run N repeated classifications for the same document/taxonomy under controlled prompt/config, record raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label changes occur only when underlying provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can occur at handoff between tokenizer/budget policy and dispatch gate (e.g., budget computed but not enforced before send). REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS required; local wiring with fake dispatcher catches it. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is pre-dispatch enforcement ('before dispatch'), testable with in-process modules.
behaviors.test_description: Wire token-budget calculator + request builder + fake dispatcher; feed over-budget and within-budget documents and assert policy outcome (truncate/reject/allow) and that disallowed requests are never dispatched.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires a real provider call and provider-side token accounting; mocks can trivially pass and hide real over-budget outcomes. Per LLM-DEP guidance, execution through a real AI/ML API defaults to Agentic unless exclusively testing wrapper plumbing with stubs. Here the assertion is about real provider-reported usage, so real model service behavior is part of correctness. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because this is a single classification call metric, not multi-stage agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run live classification on representative documents, collect provider-reported token usage metadata per call, and assert usage <= configured budget; fail on any budget exceedance.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: retry behavior is boundary logic across API client + retry/backoff policy + caller return path. In-process fake API responses (e.g., 429 then success) can reproduce the real failure. REAL-INFRA: real network/subprocess is not required to validate control flow. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output content. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is protocol/control-flow handling ('signals rate too high, waits and retries'), which deterministic stubs can faithfully trigger.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client scripted to return rate-limit signal then success; verify module delays/backs off per policy, retries, and ultimately returns a successful label instead of surfacing an immediate error.
